items.title: [正面] In my opinion it's one of the better card game sty...
items.summary: In my opinion it's one of the better card game styled gacha games out there. The art is amazing and the story in a little dark maybe for some people, but it's interesting. The strategies aren't too hard to figure out and overall just a fun game to play. Would recommend this to people without a doubt.
items.review: In my opinion it's one of the better card game styled gacha games out there. The art is amazing and the story in a little dark maybe for some people, but it's interesting. The strategies aren't too hard to figure out and overall just a fun game to play. Would recommend this to people without a doubt.

items.title: [正面] I cannot tell you just how much i love this game....
items.summary:
I cannot tell you just how much i love this game. I almost passed over it two years ago when I first saw it, and I am so glad I didn't.
In the beginning it was lacking in human translators, character background story/side story, among other things, but that was because the Studio was deeply in the red, and they wanted to deliver a satisfying finish to the story before they laid the project down. So they had to cut *everything* that was unrelated to the main story. They even stated that they had to squish three arcs' worth of story into three chapters!
After chapter 9 was completed, the point where they planned to lay it to rest, support absolutely exploded because people spread news about the game across several platforms, and thus they were able to continue.

If you look closely, if you look at all the characters' designs, look at their 'Archives' tab for each Awakener, seeing the story and the small chats the Awakeners have with the Keeper, you can see just how much love the Studio put in to each and every character.
And the art...oh, the art. The fact that they manage to come up with such incredible designs, not only for the characters, but also the backgrounds, and the Wheel of Destinys, with such a small team, truly astounds me.
Not only that, the story they have created, the world they've made is so deep, and so interconnected. It drew me in and it will not let me go.

They have made rewards easier to get, to make the gacha aspect so much kinder than other gacha games, and increased Awakener ability in combat to make the game easier on more casual players, along with actually making whole chapters easier. The Hard Mode is there for people who want an extra challenge, with adequate rewards there as well.
All of this, and the fact that they are getting out of the red, getting to a better financial standing, which, in the end, means even better gameplay, more features and side stories, extra challenges for extra rewards, truly excites me.

This game is such a gem. I have loved every second of it, seeing obscure references in some parts of the game to previous parts, making connections in the lore, the combat mechanics, everything.
Please, try this game out. And if you love it, and you keep playing and supporting B.I.A.V. Studio, even better.

After chapter 9 was completed, the point where they planned to lay it to rest, support absolutely exploded because people spread news about the game across several platforms, and thus they were able to continue.

If you look closely, if you look at all the characters' designs, look at their 'Archives' tab for each Awakener, seeing the story and the small chats the Awakeners have with the Keeper, you can see just how much love the Studio put in to each and every character.
And the art...oh, the art. The fact that they manage to come up with such incredible designs, not only for the characters, but also the backgrounds, and the Wheel of Destinys, with such a small team, truly astounds me.
Not only that, the story they have created, the world they've made is so deep, and so interconnected. It drew me in and it will not let me go.

They have made rewards easier to get, to make the gacha aspect so much kinder than other gacha games, and increased Awakener ability in combat to make the game easier on more casual players, along with actually making whole chapters easier. The Hard Mode is there for people who want an extra challenge, with adequate rewards there as well.
All of this, and the fact that they are getting out of the red, getting to a better financial standing, which, in the end, means even better gameplay, more features and side stories, extra challenges for extra rewards, truly excites me.

This game is such a gem. I have loved every second of it, seeing obscure references in some parts of the game to previous parts, making connections in the lore, the combat mechanics, everything.

items.title:
[负面] This thing is pure FOMO

You can get Saya, but now...
items.summary:
This thing is pure FOMO

You can get Saya, but now you have to pull for her (she used to be free) and they dont even give out the 250 free pulls anymore

You are better off playing something else instead of this FOMO festival

items.title: [正面] Kind of a run of the mill deck fighting game with...
items.summary: Kind of a run of the mill deck fighting game with gacha. Beautiful characters and design, but nothing really pushes it beyond its more notable contemporaries. Still okay tho if this is your thing.
